phrase: Only matching records
meaning: Rows that have corresponding keys in both tables

phrase: Employees who have departments
meaning: Match employees to departments; exclude employees with no department and departments with no employees

phrase: Students who submitted assignments
meaning: Match students to assignments; exclude students who haven't submitted

phrase: Customers who placed orders
meaning: Match customers to orders; exclude customers who haven't ordered yet

phrase: Return all employees even if department doesn't exist
meaning: Keep all employees; show NULL department info if unmatched

phrase: Include employees without department
meaning: Keep all employees; fill missing department columns with NULL

phrase: Keep all employees
meaning: Preserve every row in the Left (Employee) table

phrase: Department may be NULL
meaning: Keep all Left table records; matching Right records are optional

phrase: Show every department even if nobody works there
meaning: Keep all departments (Right table) and match employees if any

phrase: Keep all departments
meaning: Preserve every row in the Right (Department) table

phrase: Return everything
meaning: Keep all records from both tables; match where possible, else NULL

phrase: Include unmatched rows from both sides
meaning: Keep all rows from both tables, filling mismatches with NULL

phrase: Keep all rows from both tables
meaning: Preserve all records from both Left and Right tables

phrase: Intersection of both datasets
meaning: Only rows present in both tables

phrase: Both tables must have a match
meaning: Exclude rows from either table that lack a corresponding key

phrase: Active users with completed transactions
meaning: Only show users who have matches in transactions table

phrase: Authors who have published books
meaning: Exclude authors with no books, and exclude books with no author details

phrase: Show products that have sales history
meaning: Only show products that match a sale transaction key

phrase: List patients who have appointments
meaning: Exclude patients with no appointments and appointments with no patients

phrase: Show orders that have been shipped by a shipper
meaning: Match orders and shippers; exclude unmatched records

phrase: Display courses that have enrolled students
meaning: Only show courses matching at least one student enrollment

phrase: Find matching rows in both sources
meaning: Rows with keys existing in both systems

phrase: Show users and their active sessions
meaning: Exclude users with no sessions and orphaned sessions

phrase: Retrieve drivers with assigned cars
meaning: Exclude drivers without cars and cars without drivers

phrase: Show students with their grades
meaning: Match student to grade record; exclude students with no grade

phrase: Match orders with invoices
meaning: Only return orders that have been invoiced

phrase: Find suppliers for our inventory items
meaning: Only show inventory items with a matching supplier

phrase: Show transactions belonging to accounts
meaning: Only matching transactions and accounts

phrase: Display employees and their projects
meaning: Only show employees assigned to projects and vice versa

phrase: List matches between A and B
meaning: Strict inner match

phrase: All employees and their department details if any
meaning: Show all employees; department info is optional

phrase: Show all customers and their orders if they have placed any
meaning: All customers; orders are optional (NULL if none)

phrase: List all products and their sales details, including unsold products
meaning: All products; sales details are optional

phrase: Include users who have not logged in
meaning: Keep all users; session details can be NULL

phrase: Retrieve all authors and their books (show NULL for authors with no books)
meaning: Keep all authors; books are optional

phrase: Show every student and their enrolled course (if any)
meaning: Keep all students; courses are optional

phrase: List all patients and their appointments (even if they have none scheduled)
meaning: Keep all patients; appointments are optional

phrase: All flights and passenger details (include empty flights)
meaning: Keep all flights; passengers are optional

phrase: Show all accounts and their transactions, including inactive accounts
meaning: Keep all accounts; transactions are optional

phrase: List every team and their members, including teams with zero members
meaning: Keep all teams; members are optional

phrase: Show all departments, along with working employees (if any)
meaning: Keep all departments (placed on Left) and working employees

phrase: List all properties, including those not currently rented
meaning: Keep all properties; rental details can be NULL

phrase: Include applicants who haven't submitted documents
meaning: Keep all applicants; documents are optional

phrase: List every vendor and their products, including vendors with no products
meaning: Keep all vendors; products are optional

phrase: Show all courses, including those with zero student registrations
meaning: Keep all courses; registrations are optional

phrase: All departments, and employees assigned to them (even if empty)
meaning: Show all departments, regardless of employee count

phrase: List all events and registration count, including events with no sign-ups
meaning: Keep all events; registrations are optional

phrase: Include items not matched in the second table
meaning: Keep all rows from the Left table

phrase: All records from the Left side plus matches from the Right
meaning: Standard definition of Left Outer Join

phrase: Retain all rows from Table A
meaning: Keep everything in Table A (Left table)

phrase: All departments, and employees if there are any working there
meaning: If departments is the Right table, show all departments

phrase: Retrieve all orders and their customer names (include orders with no customer account)
meaning: If orders is Right table, keep all orders; customer is optional

phrase: List all assets, showing assigned employees (even if unassigned)
meaning: If assets is Right table, keep all assets

phrase: Keep all rows in Table B, matching Table A
meaning: Preserve Right table (Table B)

phrase: Show every project, along with assigned employees (include empty projects)
meaning: If projects is Right table, keep all projects

phrase: List all categories and products (include categories with no products)
meaning: If categories is Right table, keep all categories

phrase: Include all stores and their sales (even stores with no sales)
meaning: If stores is Right table, keep all stores

phrase: All courses and student enrollments (include courses with no enrollments)
meaning: If courses is Right table, keep all courses

phrase: Keep every row from the second table
meaning: Preserve all rows in the Right table

phrase: Retrieve all shippers and their shipped orders, including shippers with no orders
meaning: If shippers is Right table, keep all shippers

phrase: Show every publisher and books they published (including publishers with no books)
meaning: If publishers is Right table, keep all publishers

phrase: All regions and their sales figures (include regions with no sales record)
meaning: If regions is Right table, keep all regions

phrase: Show all doctors and their patient logs, even doctors who haven't seen patients
meaning: If doctors is Right table, keep all doctors

phrase: Keep all Right table records
meaning: Preserve all records from the Right table

phrase: Right Outer Join
meaning: Return all rows from Right table, plus matches from Left table

phrase: Show all employees and all departments, unmatched on both sides included
meaning: Keep all employees and all departments; fill mismatches with NULL

phrase: Complete outer join
meaning: Union of Left Outer Join and Right Outer Join

phrase: Show all customers and all orders, including unmatched customers and orders
meaning: Keep all customers and all orders

phrase: All products and all sales promotions, matching or not
meaning: Keep all products and all promotions

phrase: Include all unmatched rows from both tables
meaning: Show everything from both Left and Right tables

phrase: Show all students and all courses, even if students aren't enrolled or courses are empty
meaning: Keep all students and courses

phrase: Both tables are preserved entirely
meaning: No records are filtered out from either side

phrase: Full Outer Join
meaning: Keep every row from both tables

phrase: Union of matching and non-matching rows from both sides
meaning: Complete outer dataset matching

phrase: Display all users and all subscription plans (active, inactive, or empty)
meaning: Keep all users and all subscription plans

phrase: Show all projects and all resources, regardless of assignment status
meaning: Keep all projects and all resources

phrase: List all branches and all managers, even branches without managers and managers without branches
meaning: Keep all branches and managers

phrase: Retrieve all orders and all shipments, including order-less shipments and shipment-less orders
meaning: Keep all orders and shipments

phrase: Total outer join
meaning: Preserves both tables entirely

phrase: All rows from A and B, matching where matching, NULL otherwise
meaning: Standard Full Outer Join logical description

phrase: All possible combinations of rows
meaning: Produce Cartesian product of both tables

phrase: Cartesian product
meaning: Every row of Left table paired with every row of Right table

phrase: Every combination of sizes and colors
meaning: Generate all possible size-color pairs (Cartesian product)

phrase: List all products against all store locations
meaning: Generate all store-product combinations

phrase: Cross join
meaning: Every row of A matched with every row of B

phrase: Permutations of two datasets
meaning: All combinations without a join filter

phrase: Generate all pairs
meaning: Cartesian product of Left and Right tables

phrase: Compare a table to itself
meaning: Join a table on a different alias of itself

phrase: Find employees and their managers
meaning: Join employee table with itself on manager_id = employee_id

phrase: Find pairs of students in the same class
meaning: Self-join student table on class_id with student_id != student_id

phrase: Find duplicate records within a table
meaning: Self-join on duplicate fields with unique id mismatch

phrase: Retrieve organizational hierarchy
meaning: Self-join to find supervisor-subordinate relationships

phrase: Find products with the same price
meaning: Self-join product table on price with different ids

phrase: Self match
meaning: Join table A with itself

phrase: Equi-join on columns with the same name
meaning: Match columns having identical names automatically

phrase: Join using specific column name in parentheses
meaning: Short syntax for joining identical column name e.g. USING(id)

phrase: Automatic match of common column names
meaning: NATURAL JOIN matches all fields with matching names

phrase: Clean output without duplicate column names
meaning: USING() or NATURAL JOIN filters out duplicate columns in SELECT *

phrase: Shortcut for ON a.id = b.id when columns match
meaning: Use USING(id) to simplify syntax

phrase: Natural match on key
meaning: NATURAL JOIN matches common column names
